title: Llama7B mixed/int8 quality-backed frontier audit
hypothesis: The mixed/int8 energy frontier must be revised after broad native 7B attention-shadow quality rejected low-precision score/softmax quantization and bounded generation/NLL quality accepted score32_float. The score32_float direction should drive the next quality-backed recost before throughput, energy, and area are ranked.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Which mixed/int8 energy rows remain rankable after the broad native and bounded generation/NLL quality gates?
include: consume merged mixed/int8 energy closure r2
consume merged broad native 7B attention-shadow quality r2
consume merged bounded generation/NLL quality r3 for score32_float
identify quality-passing candidate IDs and precision profile
invalidate score/softmax-quantized energy rows that do not match the passing precision point
record the score32_float recost requirement before throughput, energy, and area ranking

expected_benefit: prevent stale score/softmax-quantized PPA rows from being ranked as the mixed/int8 frontier
preserve the old energy row as an unranked latency/traffic floor while identifying the required recost
define the next PPA job around score32_float with high-precision or exact score-softmax
risks: this audit does not produce the recosted PPA numbers
the generation/NLL gate remains bounded and is not full downstream task accuracy
the passing score32_float direction still needs a hardware score-softmax embodiment before final comparison

required_evaluations.objective: Reconcile mixed/int8 energy closure against broad native 7B quality and score32 generation/NLL evidence, then mark non-matching energy rows unrankable.

expected_result.reason: The bounded generation/NLL result passes score32_float; the old low-precision score/softmax energy-best row must not remain a quality-backed frontier point until the score32 path is recosted.
